title: Action Context — Enriched Write Command Responses
overview: Add an opt-in action context mode to browse that appends a compact state delta to every write command response. When enabled, agents receive a one-line summary of what changed (URL, title, dialog, tab count, console errors) after each action — eliminating the need for expensive snapshot calls between steps. Reduces agent token consumption by ~3x for typical multi-step workflows.
scopeChallenge: Considered live dashboard (agent-browser style), session memory commands, terminal-rendered viewport. All rejected — highest-value, lowest-friction approach is enriching existing command responses. No new commands to learn, no new workflow. Opt-in via existing X-Browse-* header pattern and --context CLI flag.

existingCodeLeverage.subProblem: Page state getters (URL, title, tabs, dialog)

failureModes.risk: page.title() is async and slow on complex pages
failureModes.mitigation: Use page.title() with 100ms timeout; fall back to empty string. Cache title in BrowserManager if needed.

failureModes.risk: Context capture adds latency to every write command
failureModes.mitigation: Only capture when opt-in flag is set. Profile before/after. Target <5ms overhead.

failureModes.risk: Console error count can be very large (50K buffer)
failureModes.mitigation: Count only errors added since last capture timestamp, not entire buffer scan.

failureModes.risk: MCP always-on context may surprise existing integrations
failureModes.mitigation: Start with opt-in for MCP too (env var BROWSE_MCP_CONTEXT=1), make default in next major.

failureModes.risk: Context line parsing breaks JSON mode consumers
failureModes.mitigation: Context is appended to result string BEFORE JSON wrapping — JSON consumers get it in data field, no parsing change.

failureModes.risk: Stale state from concurrent commands in same session
failureModes.mitigation: State capture is synchronous read of in-memory values; no race condition possible. Test with rapid sequential calls.

testCoverageMap.codepath: server.ts context injection for write commands
server.ts no context for read/meta commands
MCP context injection for write tools
MCP no context for read/meta tools

tasks.title: Define PageState interface and context types
tasks.description: Create TypeScript types for page state capture and context delta. Add to existing src/types.ts which has CommandContext and CommandResult. Define PageState (url, title, tabCount, dialog, consoleErrorCount, networkPendingCount, timestamp) and ContextDelta (urlChanged?, titleChanged?, dialogAppeared?, dialogDismissed?, tabsChanged?, consoleErrors?, navigated?). Also add contextEnabled?: boolean to Session interface in session-manager.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: PageState interface defined with fields: url, title, tabCount, dialog (type+message | null), consoleErrorCount, networkPendingCount, timestamp
ContextDelta interface defined with optional change fields
Session interface in session-manager.ts extended with contextEnabled?: boolean
TypeScript compiles cleanly with tsc --noEmit
Edge case: interfaces handle null/undefined dialog state correctly

tasks.title: Implement capturePageState() function
tasks.description: Create src/action-context.ts with async capturePageState(bm: BrowserManager, buffers: SessionBuffers): Promise<PageState>. Reads URL from bm.getCurrentUrl(), title from bm.getPage().title() with 100ms timeout, tabs from bm.getTabCount(), dialog from bm.getLastDialog(), console error count from buffers. Must be fast (<5ms typical). Handle edge cases: about:blank, closed pages, crashed contexts.
acceptanceCriteria: async capturePageState() captures all PageState fields from BrowserManager and SessionBuffers
Execution time <5ms typical (page.title() with 100ms timeout, fallback to empty string)
Uses bm.getLastDialog() for dialog state
Gracefully handles about:blank pages, closed pages, crashed contexts — returns partial state with defaults
Returns consoleErrorCount by counting error-level entries added since last capture

tasks.title: Implement buildContextDelta() and formatContextLine()
tasks.description: In src/action-context.ts, add buildContextDelta(before: PageState, after: PageState): ContextDelta that diffs two states and formatContextLine(delta: ContextDelta): string that produces a compact one-line summary. Format: ' | /cart → /checkout | title: Checkout | +dialog: confirm'. Returns empty string when nothing changed.
acceptanceCriteria: buildContextDelta() detects URL change, title change, dialog appear/dismiss, tab count change, new console errors
formatContextLine() produces compact output like ' | /cart → /checkout | title: Checkout | +dialog: confirm'
Output is token-efficient: <100 chars for typical single-change scenarios
Returns empty string when both states identical
Multiple changes in one action all included in one line

tasks.title: Wire action context into server.ts command pipeline
tasks.description: In server.ts handleCommand(), add before/after state capture around write command execution when actionContext option is enabled. Extend RequestOptions with actionContext: boolean, read from x-browse-context header. After write command, compute delta and append formatted context line to result string. Do NOT capture for read/meta commands. Zero overhead when disabled.
acceptanceCriteria: RequestOptions extended with actionContext: boolean from x-browse-context header
Before write command: capturePageState() called; after: second capture + delta appended to result
Context NOT gathered for read/meta commands
Context NOT gathered when actionContext is false (zero overhead)
If write command throws, no context logic runs (no try/catch complexity)

tasks.title: Add --context CLI flag
tasks.description: In cli.ts, add --context flag to cliFlags parsing (alongside --json, --content-boundaries). When set, adds X-Browse-Context: 1 header to all requests in sendCommand(). Update help text to include the new flag.
acceptanceCriteria: --context flag parsed in CLI flags section alongside --json, --max-output
When set, adds X-Browse-Context: 1 header in sendCommand()
Help text includes --context in flags list
Flag works in combination with --json (context appended before JSON wrapping)

tasks.title: Add action context support to MCP server
tasks.description: In mcp.ts CallToolRequestSchema handler, add before/after state capture around write command execution. Import capturePageState and buildContextDelta/formatContextLine from action-context.ts. Append context line to result text for write commands only. Start opt-in via BROWSE_MCP_CONTEXT=1 env var.
acceptanceCriteria: MCP write command results include context delta line when state changes
MCP read/meta commands do NOT include context
Context appended to text content, not as separate content block
Works correctly with existing JSON mode wrapping in MCP
snapshot command returns refs resource without context added

tasks.title: Integration tests for action context (HTTP server)
tasks.description: Write integration tests covering context deltas across write command scenarios. Test in test/features.test.ts (add Action Context section) using the shared BrowserManager and test fixtures. Cover: goto (URL+title), click with navigation, fill (no change), dialog trigger, empty context, JSON mode combination.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: goto produces context with URL and title
Test: click on link produces context with URL change
Test: fill on input produces empty context (no state change)
Test: action triggering dialog produces context with dialog info
Test: context is empty string when nothing changes
Test: context works with --json mode
Edge case: rapid sequential commands don't produce stale state

tasks.title: MCP integration tests for action context
tasks.description: Add tests to test/mcp.test.ts verifying action context through MCP tool calls. Test browse_click returns context, browse_snapshot does not, browse_fill with no change is clean, JSON mode wrapping works.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: MCP browse_click returns result with context line
Test: MCP browse_snapshot does NOT include context
Test: MCP browse_fill with no state change returns clean result
Edge case: MCP JSON mode wraps context-enriched result correctly

tasks.title: Add browse set context on/off session command
tasks.description: In src/commands/write.ts, extend the existing 'set' command case to support 'context on', 'context off', and 'context' (status). The contextEnabled field is already on the Session interface (added in TASK-001). Read/write it via the session object. Session-level flag overrides per-request header in server.ts. Must not affect other sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: browse set context on enables action context for current session
browse set context off disables action context for current session
browse set context (no value) returns current status
Session-level flag overrides per-request header
set context on in one session doesn't affect other sessions

tasks.title: Update MCP tool descriptions for context awareness
tasks.description: Update write command tool descriptions in src/mcp-tools.ts to mention enriched responses with state context. browse_click, browse_goto, browse_fill etc. should note that responses include page state changes when context is enabled.
acceptanceCriteria: Write command descriptions mention state context in responses
browse_click description mentions: returns click confirmation plus page state changes
No read command descriptions modified
Descriptions stay under 300 chars

tasks.title: Documentation — action context guide
tasks.description: Create MDX doc in the website repo at content/docs/guides/action-context.mdx explaining the feature. Cover: the problem (token waste on snapshots), before/after examples, three activation methods (--context, MCP, set context on), delta fields, best practices.
acceptanceCriteria: Explains the problem (agents waste tokens on snapshots between actions)
Shows before/after examples of output with and without context
Documents all three activation methods
Lists all context delta fields with examples
Notes that context only added when state actually changes
